columns.companiesTooltip: How many companies run at least one ship with this exact design.
columns.shipsTooltip: Total ships built to this design across all companies.
columns.weightTooltip: Empty weight of the ship, derived from its components. This decides which bridge and hull plate tier it needs.
columns.bridgeTooltip: Bridge tier required, derived from empty weight. Not chosen directly.
columns.hullTooltip: Hull plate tier required, derived from empty weight. Not chosen directly.
columns.shieldingTooltip: Heat / radiation shielding, as a pair.
columns.ratioTooltip: Tonnes of cargo per emitter. Higher is usually cheaper per ton but slower.
columns.costPerTonTooltip: Fuel and repair kits for the loaded trip, divided by the cargo carried. Priced with your own perks and prices.
columns.flightTimeTooltip: One-way loaded travel time in minutes, at the chosen reactor mode.
columns.flightCostTooltip: Total credits for the loaded trip, before dividing by tonnage.
columns.buildCostTooltip: Construction cost at your prices. Materials come from the server, so the formula matches the game.
columns.upgradeCostTooltip: Cost to rebuild the selected ship as this design, at your prices. Only materials you would have to buy: upgrading destroys the components it replaces, so nothing comes back.
columns.perksTooltip: Ship perks the companies flying this design hold. Dimmed when fewer than half of them do. The number on an icon is the typical level.
columns.cargoTooltip: Boosted by your own Packing Standards and Haul and Crawl.
columns.cargoCommunityTooltip: Boosted by the cargo perks a majority of this design's companies hold. The range filter still compares against the published capacity, since the boost differs per design.

designs.nearThreshold: Close to {{weight}} empty weight, where this design crosses into {{what}}. Upgrading past it strands the lower-tier parts already built.
designs.starlifter: Also requires Starlifter Elements (empty weight is 15,000 or more).
designs.openInShips: Open this design on the Ships page in a new tab

costRanking.noRouteHint: Set a route and cargo amount to price these columns.
costRanking.tooManyHint: Narrow the filters to {{max}} designs or fewer to sort and filter by this.
costRanking.tooMany: {{total}} designs match, which is more than the {{max}} that can be ranked by cost at once. The figures below are for this page only. Narrow the filters, by emitter type or cargo range for example, to rank them all.

designs.cargoBoosted: {{base}} t published, +{{bonus}}% from your cargo perks
designs.cargoBoostedCommunity: {{base}} t published, +{{bonus}}% from its companies' cargo perks
designs.perkNotCounted: Not counted: held by fewer than half
designs.perkUsesYours: Figures shown use your own perks

components.clickToFilter: Filter every other block by this
components.cargoShare: Share of ships inside this cargo range
components.perkShare: Share of ships in view whose company is in the selected levels

achievements.intro: There are a few things hidden around the app. Find one and it shows up here.
achievements.minigamesHelp: Little games hidden across the app. Find one and it unlocks here, with its own leaderboard.
achievements.discoveriesHelp: Things hidden across the app, mostly community lore and inside jokes. Find one and it turns up here.

route.buttonHint: Route, cargo and reactor mode used for the cost figures. Leave cargo empty to load each design to capacity.

modes.cheapestHint: Lowest cost per ton, however long it takes.
modes.bestHint: Lowest cost multiplied by time, so it pays for speed when speed is worth it.

shipInfo.unpriced: {{count}} material without a price, so the total is low.
shipInfo.unpriced_other: {{count}} materials without a price, so the total is low.
shipInfo.setRoute: Set a route and cargo amount to price this design.
shipInfo.notFlyable: This design cannot complete the route.
shipInfo.costPerTonTooltip: Fuel and repair kits for the loaded trip, per ton carried.
shipInfo.roundTripTooltip: Out loaded and back empty. This is what caps how often the ship can run the route.
shipInfo.efficiencyTooltip: {{efficiency}}% efficiency, {{power}}% power. Lower power burns less fuel per light-year but takes longer.
shipInfo.adoptionTooltip: Ships built to this design and how many companies run one.
shipInfo.fuelTooltip: Fuel for the loaded trip against tank capacity. Amber: a round trip will not fit. Red: one leg will not.
shipInfo.cargoLoadedCappedTooltip: This design is full. Its capacity is below the cargo amount you set, or you set none.
shipInfo.cargoLoadedRequestedTooltip: The cargo amount from your flight settings.

upgrade.pickShipHint: Pick one of your ships to see what upgrading to each design costs.

perkBasis.label: Perks:
perkBasis.youHint: Capacity and speed as your own Packing Standards and Haul and Crawl give them.
perkBasis.communityHint: Capacity and speed as the companies flying each design get them, using the perks a majority of them hold.
perkBasis.alwaysYours: Prices, your guild Flight Center, Reinforced Hulls and Fuel Efficiency are always yours: they scale every design alike, so they never change which one is best.

science.won: The chain is complete. Higher grades open up from here.

roboChicken.blurb: Not available in your region. Or any region.
roboChicken.note: No known buyer, so the economics are left as an exercise for the reader.

discoveries.title: Discoveries
discoveries.onlyYou: Only you have found this so far

industries.intro: Which research fields the community invests in, how specialized companies are and which field combinations are most common. Industry is read from research levels alone, anonymously and in aggregate.

meta.empty: No community data yet. Check back once companies have contributed.

growth.footnote: Company-value growth of single-spec companies aged 14 days or more that were active in the last day. A revealed signal on a subset, so it leans toward where strong players are.
growth.empty: Not enough companies yet to show growth by tech.

specialization.subtitle: How many fields each company is specialized in.
specialization.rule: How specialized each company is. Its most-researched field always counts as a spec; a further field counts only when it is leveled up meaningfully too, roughly a third of the top field's level or more, so a few stray points don't. Single, dual and diversified mean one, two, and three or more specs; companies with no research yet are their own group.

singleSpec.subtitle: Of single-spec companies, the field they focus on.

combos.subtitle: Field combinations companies specialize in, most common first.

meta.empty: No community data yet. Check back once companies have contributed.

adoption.footnote: The bar is the share of contributing companies that hold each perk at any level. The box plot on the right shows the level distribution among holders, on each perk's own 1-to-max range: the box is the middle 50%, the tick is the median and the thin line is the full range. Single-level perks have no box plot.

exclusive.subtitle: Groups where a company picks exactly one perk. Bars include a 'none' bucket, so they sum to the whole community.

economic.footnote: Company value of the companies holding each perk, active in the last day and aged 14 days or more. Company value is public; a perk is only shown once enough holders back it. Bigger companies naturally hold more perks, so read this as association, not cause.
economic.empty: Not enough companies yet to show value by perk.

industry.footnote: Among companies specialized in a single research field, the perks they hold as a share of that industry, with the community-wide adoption drawn as the baseline tick. Switch between fixing an industry and fixing a perk.
industry.empty: Not enough single-spec companies yet to break perks down by industry.
